reasoning: Reject — wrong domain entirely. This candidate is a Salesforce Copado DevOps Engineer with zero evidence of embedded C programming, MISRA guidelines, Autosar, RTOS, RADAR systems, or ADAS — the non-negotiable core requirements of this Senior SW Developer role. Her entire 6-year career is in Salesforce CI/CD release management, which shares no meaningful overlap with automotive/embedded software development. Not a single must-have skill is met. Do not proceed with this profile under any circumstances.
strengths: COPADO CI/CD MASTERY: Delivered a quantified 30% reduction in deployment time through Copado SFP and Metadata Format pipelines at Trimble Inc., with hands-on CLI operations and Agentforce AI component deployments — strong signal for Salesforce DevOps roles, entirely irrelevant here.

RELEASE GOVERNANCE AT ENTERPRISE SCALE: Managed multi-environment production releases with 24/7 support, risk assessments, and post-deployment validation across telecom-domain clients at Atos Syntel for 4+ years — demonstrates senior-level ownership, but in the wrong domain.

CERTIFICATIONS IN SALESFORCE ECOSYSTEM: Holds 6 certifications including Copado Fundamentals I and II (both SFP and Metadata formats) and Salesforce Platform Developer I — strong credential stack, but all are Salesforce-specific and offer zero transferability to embedded automotive development.
gaps: ZERO EMBEDDED/AUTOMOTIVE TECHNICAL STACK: The candidate has no exposure to C programming, MISRA coding standards, Autosar, or RTOS — these are non-negotiable hard requirements for this role. There is no partial match or transferable skill. This is a complete domain mismatch.

NO RADAR OR ADAS DOMAIN KNOWLEDGE: The JD explicitly requires RADAR system knowledge and flags ADAS as a strong advantage. The candidate's entire background is in Salesforce CRM/DevOps with telecom domain clients — no signal of any automotive, embedded, or sensor-system experience whatsoever.

scoring_factors.reason: No notice period specified in the resume; no JD maximum stated — no penalty applied.
JD requires in-office presence in Chennai; candidate is based in Puducherry, India — location does not match.
Candidate has 6+ years total IT experience and 4+ years relevant Salesforce experience — above the half-of-minimum threshold numerically, though in an entirely different domain.
JD gender preference is 'Any' — no penalty applied.
No college exclusion or preference specified in the JD — no penalty or bonus applied.

availability_reason: Current role at Trimble Inc. is listed as March 2025 to Present with no end date or notice period stated on the resume.
alternative_role_fit: Senior Salesforce Release Engineer / Salesforce DevOps Engineer
rare_assets: Dual Copado certification tracks (both SFP and Metadata Format) — uncommon even among Salesforce DevOps specialists.
Agentforce AI component deployments at Trimble Inc. — early adopter signal in an emerging Salesforce AI toolchain.
self_reported_unverified: 30% reduction in deployment time — no baseline metrics, team size, or deployment volume provided to validate the magnitude of this claim.
'Bronze Accolade Award' and 'Spot Recognition Award' — internal awards with no external verification or context on selection criteria or competition pool.
interview_flags: Clarify why this candidate applied for an embedded C/automotive role — potential misapplication or resume misrouted.
Probe whether candidate has any background in C or embedded systems not reflected on the CV.
Ask about the MBA in HR Management (completing December 2025) — does this signal a planned career pivot away from technical roles?
Verify actual hands-on depth with Copado CLI vs. UI-driven deployments — resume blends both without distinguishing technical depth.
Clarify team size managed and whether the 'Senior' title reflected independent ownership or was a band-level designation at Atos Syntel.
